domain: Japanese craft (kintsugi: urushi+gold lacquer repair of broken pottery; wabi-sabi philosophy of visible imperfection)
specific_mechanism: Kintsugi repairs broken pottery by filling cracks with urushi lacquer mixed with gold/silver/platinum powder. The repair is INTENTIONALLY visible and accentuated — the crack becomes part of the object's history. Wabi-sabi philosophy: imperfection, impermanence, and the trace of repair are aesthetic features, not flaws. The repair material is both functional (urushi durable+water-resistant) and signaling (gold makes the scar legible).
llm_application: Kintsugi-style 'visible-scar' error trace: instead of suppressing model errors or unlearning behavior with detectable but unlabeled traces (2506.14003 unlearning-isn't-invisible), explicitly INSCRIBE the correction trajectory with a 'gold lacquer' annotation in the model's output stream — every post-hoc fix carries a structured annotation that says 'this part of the response was corrected from <original> to <revised> at <step>'. Phase coherence: the inscribed-trace annotation phase-locks to the original-output phase, preserving the model's history rather than overwriting it.
novelty_claim: Unlearning-isn't-invisible (2506.14003), MAST taxonomy (2503.13657), Demystifying-Errors (2512.00215), LLM Reasoning Failures (2602.06176), Backward-Spreading-to-Forward-Replay (2605.00358) all study LLM error traces and post-hoc interventions. None specifically proposes deliberate kintsugi-style explicit inscription of correction trajectories as a visible aesthetic+functional feature of the output.
